entries.notes: UMBRELLA entry covering the entire Nerite snail trade group. alsoKnownAs: Zebra Nerite (Neritina natalensis), Tiger Nerite (Neritina semiconca), Olive Nerite (Neritina reclivata), Horned Nerite (Clithon cf. diadema), Red Racer Nerite (Vittina waigiensis), King Koopa Nerite (Neritina juttingae), Spotted Nerite, Black Racer Nerite. All share identical care: algae cleanup crew, brackish-water larvae (do not breed in pure FW), tolerant of soft-to-hard FW, lay sesame-seed eggs on hardscape that don't hatch in pure FW. Hybrid policy aggressive consolidation per user instruction (mollusc-fw slice).

alsoKnownAs: Pomacea diffusa (synonym), Apple Snail (broad trade — flag confusion with the invasive P. canaliculata in a separate entry), Gold Mystery, Blue Mystery, Ivory Mystery, Magenta Mystery, Jade Mystery (color morphs share identical care). Small-to-medium apple snail safe for the planted-tank hobby; non-invasive in most jurisdictions; egg-laying above water requires a humid air gap. Distinct from P. canaliculata (separate entry — large, plant-destructive, banned in most jurisdictions).

alsoKnownAs: Channeled Apple Snail, Golden Apple Snail (trade), Pomacea maculata (sister species — folded in, similarly invasive). Large (up to 15 cm), voracious plant destroyer. Globally INVASIVE — banned for sale or possession in the EU, UK, USA (Lacey Act listed 2014), and many other jurisdictions. Research must include legal-status caveat prominently. Set hobbyistAdvisory: legally-restricted.

UMBRELLA entry covering: Planorbella duryi (most common trade), Planorbella trivolvis (Great Ramshorn), Planorbarius corneus (European Great Ramshorn), Helisoma (older genus), Marisa cornuarietis (Giant Ramshorn / Colombian Ramshorn — actually distinct biology, plant-eater, larger — covered HERE with a notes split rather than its own entry per the conservative consolidation policy; flag the plant-destruction risk for Marisa). Sells via hitchhike on plants and through dedicated trade. Color morphs: Pink/Red/Blue/Leopard Ramshorn all share care.

alsoKnownAs: MTS, Trumpet Snail, Red-rimmed Melania, Sand-Burrowing Snail. Live-bearing nocturnal sand-bed cleaner — burrows through substrate during the day, preventing anaerobic pockets. Parthenogenetic — populations explode when overfed. Beloved by some hobbyists for sand-stirring; treated as pest by others. Reef-aquarist's anti-bubble-algae substrate cleaner; planted-tank substrate health driver.

UMBRELLA entry for the small-pest-snail complex: Physella acuta (Bladder Snail), Physa spp. (genus group), Lymnaea spp. (Pond Snails — L. stagnalis, L. peregra), Galba truncatula. Almost never sold deliberately — hitchhike on plants. Frequently regarded as pests by aquarists but valuable algae/detritus cleaners in moderation. Reproduce rapidly; populations track food availability. Entry exists for identification purposes (so a hobbyist who finds one can identify and decide whether to remove).

alsoKnownAs: Clea helena (older genus), Bumblebee Snail (FW — distinct from saltwater Engina mendicaria), Pest-Snail-Eater. Predatory snail used for biological control of pest snail populations (Pond/Bladder/Ramshorn). Burrows during the day, hunts at night. Will eat dead shrimp and any soft tissue. Will NOT typically eat the much larger Mystery/Apple/Nerite snails.

UMBRELLA entry for the entire Tylomelania trade group (Sulawesi rabbit snails). Multiple species + many color/pattern morphs share effectively identical care: Tylomelania zemis (most common), Tylomelania patriarchalis, Tylomelania gemmifera, Tylomelania spp. + trade morphs (Yellow, Orange, Chocolate, Gold Spot, Spotted Black, White Spotted, Yellow Spotted). Tropical hard alkaline FW (Sulawesi lakes). Slow-growing, low-fecundity live-bearers. Compatible with Sulawesi Caridina shrimp at matched parameters.

UMBRELLA entry covering the Brotia trade genus: Brotia herculea (Hercules / White Hercules — large, ridged shell), Brotia pagodula (Pagoda — distinctive stepped pagoda-shaped shell), Brotia spp. Tropical Southeast Asian (mostly Thai) hard-alkaline-water snails. Specialty importer species (Aqua Imports). Similar Tylomelania-style care but distinct genus.

UMBRELLA entry for the Viviparidae trade group: Cipangopaludina chinensis (Chinese / Japanese Trapdoor — coldwater pond standard), Cipangopaludina japonica (Japanese Trapdoor — frequently conflated), Viviparus malleatus (alternative trade ID for Trapdoor), Viviparus sp. (Blueberry Snail morph — sold by Aqua Imports). All live-bearing, mostly coldwater-tolerant. Popular pond cleanup. Often kept in goldfish setups.
